tldr: Move from Bucket's hosted feature management to Togglz, a self-hosted Apache 2.0 Java feature toggle library backed by enums.
why_switch: Eliminate per-seat/monthly subscription cost in favor of free OSS
Keep flag evaluation in-process inside your JVM application
Type-safe flag definitions via Java enums
Reduce vendor lock-in for a critical runtime dependency
what_to_export: List of all flag keys and their human-readable names
Audit log of recent flag changes (if needed for compliance)
Currently archived/stale flags to drop during migration
export_method: Use Bucket's dashboard or API (consult official Bucket docs for the current export endpoint or CSV/JSON download) to retrieve all flags, their states, and targeting rules. If no bulk export exists, script a fetch against their REST API or copy flag definitions manually for small flag sets. Capture environment-by-environment so you can map each Togglz FeatureManager configuration cleanly.
import_method: In Togglz, declare each flag as a value in a Java enum implementing Feature, then configure activation strategies (UsernameActivationStrategy, GradualRolloutStrategy, etc.) in your FeatureManager. Persist state through one of Togglz's StateRepository implementations (JDBC, Redis, file, in-memory). For Spring Boot, use the togglz-spring-boot-starter and the admin console to set runtime states matching your exported Bucket configuration.

concept_mapping.note: One enum constant per flag; type-checked at compile time
Map percentage/user rules to built-in or custom strategies
Use separate repositories or profiles per environment
Web console ships with the Spring Boot starter
Replace SDK lookups with enum-method calls
Wire logging or DB persistence for change history

gotchas: Togglz is JVM-only; non-Java services in your stack won't be served by it
No first-class experimentation/A-B analytics, build separately if needed
Admin console must be secured before production exposure
Strategies are evaluated in-process, so config drift across instances is possible without a shared StateRepository
Migrating large flag sets benefits from a script that generates the enum and a SQL seed file

email.subject: Moving feature flags from Bucket to Togglz
email.body:
Hi team,

We're moving our feature flags from Bucket to Togglz to drop the subscription and keep flag evaluation in-process inside our JVM apps. Plan: export all flags, states, and targeting rules from Bucket, then declare them as a Java enum and configure Togglz activation strategies behind a shared StateRepository. The Spring Boot starter gives us a web console for runtime toggling. I'll cut over environments one at a time and keep both systems live during the switch. Effort estimate: 1-3 days.

Thanks
